title: Construct Binary Tree from Preorder and Inorder Traversal
description:
You're given two lists describing the same binary tree of distinct values: `preorder` (root, then left subtree, then right subtree) and `inorder` (left subtree, then root, then right subtree). Rebuild the tree and return it as a level-order list where `null` marks a missing child (root first, children filled in level by level, skipping subtrees under a `null`, trailing `null`s omitted).

**Example**

```
construct_tree([3, 9, 20, 15, 7], [9, 3, 15, 20, 7])
# ->  [3, 9, 20, null, null, 15, 7]
#
#         3
#        / \
#       9  20
#          /  \
#        15    7
```
starter_code:
def construct_tree(preorder, inorder):
    # preorder, inorder: traversal lists of the same tree (distinct values).
    # Rebuild the tree and return its level-order list.
    pass
hints: The first element of preorder is always the current subtree's root.

Find that root's position in inorder: everything to its left belongs to the left subtree, everything to its right belongs to the right subtree -- and both subtrees keep the same relative order in preorder.

Precompute a value -> index map for inorder so you can locate the root in O(1), and track a moving pointer into preorder as you consume it root-first; then recurse on the left inorder range before the right one, since preorder always advances left-subtree elements before right-subtree ones.

solutions.name: Recursive split using an inorder index map
solutions.explanation: The first unused element of preorder is always the root of the current subtree. Looking that value up in inorder tells us exactly how many nodes belong to its left subtree (everything before it) versus its right subtree (everything after it). Because preorder visits a subtree's root before any of its descendants, and always finishes the entire left subtree before touching the right subtree, we can recurse: build the left subtree over the inorder range before the root's index (consuming preorder in order as we go), then build the right subtree over the range after it. A shared pointer into preorder (advanced as each root is consumed) and a value -> index map for inorder (built once, O(1) lookups) keep this at O(n). Once the tree is reconstructed, serialize it to its level-order form.
